competitors.name: deep_research_analyst
competitors.rules: Holds the analyst's target weights; rebalanced each tick from a fresh research report (web + Robinhood data).

competitors.name: momentum_breakout
competitors.rules: Buy a 20-day-high breakout confirmed by above-average volume; exit when price closes below its 20-day SMA. Few big winners, many small losers.

trade_log.reason: fill to cap: breakout > 20d high on volume
fill to cap: breakout > 20d high on volume

competitors.name: blended_momo_rsi
competitors.rules: Momentum breakout, but skip entries already overbought (RSI ≥ 70); exit on trend break or RSI > 75. Avoids chasing extended moves.

trade_log.reason: fill to cap: breakout, RSI 64 not overbought
fill to cap: breakout, RSI 66 not overbought

competitors.name: mean_reversion
competitors.rules: Buy when RSI(14) < 30 (oversold); sell when RSI(14) > 70 (overbought). Trades less, wins more often, smaller edge.

competitors.name: mirofish_real
competitors.rules: Real-MiroFish: persona agents with memory interact over rounds; consensus picks (weight ∝ vote share) rebalanced each tick.

competitors.name: llm_voters
competitors.rules: Allocates across the swarm's top vote-getters (weight ∝ vote share, capped at 60%); rebalanced each tick.

decisions.reason: fill to cap: breakout > 20d high on volume

analyst.as_of: 2026-06-23 — daily bars settled through the 6/22 close; live 6/23 tape (semis slammed) and MU's 6/24-pm print in view. Universe screened in randomized order to avoid position bias.
analyst.sizing: De-risk into the 6/24 MU print: exit the highest-beta semis (ARM + 3x SOXL), halve MU, keep HOOD as the non-semi anchor, add a small uncorrelated MRNA flier, and raise cash to ~55% ahead of the print + Thursday PCE.

rationale.MU: Halved 30% -> 15% into the 6/24-pm print. The franchise thesis is intact — sold-out HBM, ~81% gross margin, the cleanest AI-memory proxy in the market — but the setup is binary and priced for perfection: company guide is ~$33.5B revenue / ~$19 EPS / ~81% GM, yet sell-side revenue estimates span $33.7B-$40.9B, a >$7B dispersion that means the after-close print, not the fundamentals, sets next week's price. With MU at 57x trailing P/E and a 52-wk high just set 6/22, we keep a real stake so a beat still pays, but refuse to anchor 30% of the book on one coin-flip. Cutting it in half is the size that lets us survive a soft guide and still redeploy from cash afterward.
rationale.ARM: Exited (was 20%). ARM is the richest, highest-beta expression of the same semis bet — 519x P/E, fresh-high momentum that has now snapped and slipped back below our 6/18 entry. Into a binary MU read it has the most multiple to lose on a soft guide and is the least defensible name to carry through the print. Better to cut a correlated, expensive loser than average it down ahead of the event.
rationale.SOXL: Exited (was 15%). A 3x daily-leveraged semis ETF is the wrong vehicle the day before a binary catalyst: path-dependent decay punishes any chop, and 3x leverage turns a soft MU guide into a gap we cannot risk-manage. The leverage was the right tool for a momentum week; it is exactly the wrong tool into the event that decides the week. Removing it takes the single largest source of correlated downside out of the book.
rationale.HOOD: Held at 20% — the non-semi anchor: fintech/crypto beta, no binary event this week, ~30% below its Oct high with room to run.
rationale.MRNA: New 10% — an uncorrelated flier: +23% over 21d on 2.4x volume near a fresh high, with its own (non-semi) story. Speculative (no earnings), so sized small.

analyst.framework: Claude for Financial Services — equity-research methodology (/screen, /sector, /comps, /catalysts, /thesis). Data: Robinhood fundamentals + web_search (no paid-vendor MCP connectors).

regime.label: Hawkish-pause Fed and a tech tape cracking into a binary semis catalyst
regime.note: Chair Kevin Warsh's Fed is squarely focused on the inflation side of the mandate — core PCE has settled above 3% (April core services ex-housing 3.5% y/y), well over the 2% target, with the AI buildout itself now a visible inflation driver. The week's two pivots are MU's 6/24-pm print (the single clearest test of whether the HBM/AI-memory supercycle is a durable franchise or a cycle that ran ahead of itself) and Thursday's May PCE, the Fed's preferred gauge. After a ~70% MU run in 2026 the whole memory/semis complex is priced for perfection, and on 6/23 it cracked — chips were slammed across the board the day before the catalyst. The disciplined response is to stop pressing the correlated bet and rebuild dry powder before the print resolves the uncertainty.

analyst.thesis: What changed since last tick: nothing about Micron's franchise, everything about the risk/reward of pressing it. Last tick built a one-week-upside book that put ~65% of equity into MU + ARM + 3x SOXL — one correlated bet on a single after-the-close print — with only ~15% cash. The prior reflection already named that as the mistake-in-waiting ('treat MU/ARM/SOXL as one correlated bet; if MU guides soft, trim the three together') and pre-committed to acting. This tick executes that plan. The 6/24-pm print is genuinely binary: company guidance (~$33.5B rev, ~$19 EPS, ~81% GM) sits inside a sell-side revenue range of $33.7B-$40.9B — a >$7B spread that says the market itself does not know whether HBM is a structural franchise or a memory cycle that has overshot, and MU at 57x trailing into a fresh 52-wk high is priced for the bullish answer. Holding a 30% anchor + 20% of the highest-beta IP name (ARM, 519x) + 15% of a 3x-leveraged ETF into that, while the tape is already gapping the whole complex down on 6/23, is gambling, not research. So we de-risk on principle, not on hindsight: exit ARM and SOXL outright (the richest and the most leveraged expressions — the most to lose and the least controllable on a soft guide), halve MU to 15% (keep a real stake because a beat on sold-out HBM still reprices it, but no binary anchor), and keep HOOD at 20% as the non-semi stabilizer — fintech/crypto beta with no event this week and room below its old high. A small 10% MRNA adds an uncorrelated, name-specific momentum flier (its own story, no semis correlation). The remaining ~55% sits in cash as deliberate dry powder for the macro tail (Thursday's PCE) and, more importantly, to redeploy into clarity once the print is out. We are not calling the print; we are sizing so that either outcome is survivable and a good entry follows.

evidence.point: MU reports fiscal Q3 after the close Wed 6/24 (verified). Company guide ~$33.5B revenue (±$750M), non-GAAP EPS ~$19.15, GM ~81%; consensus has crept to ~$35.4B / ~$20.2-20.4 EPS — but analyst revenue estimates span $33.7B-$40.9B, a >$7B dispersion = deep uncertainty on the HBM ramp.
The print is framed as the single clearest test of whether the ~70%-in-2026 HBM run is a durable, structural franchise or a memory cycle that has run ahead of itself.

Live 6/23 (decision day): the semis complex was slammed across the board the day before the catalyst — MU -13% (to ~$1051 vs the $1211 6/22 close), 3x SOXL -23%, ARM -10%, MRVL -9%, TSM -7%, AMD -6%; HOOD held up best at -2.4%, MRNA was green +2.7%.

Valuation into the print: MU 57x P/E at a 52-wk high set 6/22; ARM 519x P/E; SOXL is 3x daily-leveraged semis (path-dependent decay). These are the priced-for-perfection, hardest-to-risk-manage names on a soft guide.

Macro swing factor: Warsh Fed focused on inflation; core PCE >3% (April core services ex-housing 3.5% y/y) vs a 2% target; May PCE prints Thursday 6/25 — a hot read hits exactly these extended, long-duration names.
MRNA screen: +23% 21d, ~2.4x recent volume surge, near a fresh 52-wk high and green on a red semi tape — an uncorrelated, name-specific momentum flier (negative P/E, so speculative and sized small).

risks: Cut-too-early risk: if MU beats and guides HBM/2027 supply up, the complex rips and we left upside on the table by exiting ARM/SOXL and halving MU — the price of refusing a binary anchor.
Cash drag: ~55% cash underperforms badly if the 6/23 selloff was the shakeout and the tape V-bounces through the print; the hedge only pays if the print or PCE disappoints.
MRNA has no earnings (negative P/E) and is a momentum flier — the 10% can round-trip fast if the move was a one-day spike; it is sized small for exactly that reason.

Execution timing: the paper book fills at the 6/22 settled closes, so the de-risk trades print before the 6/23 drop reaches the book; the decision stands on the 6/22-known setup (binary print, priced-for-perfection, 65% correlated, thin cash), not on the 6/23 tape, which is confirmation.

data_examined.label: Screen: 5d/10d/21d/63d/126d momentum, 20d-SMA distance, 6-mo range position, 5d-vs-20d volume surge across the 100-name universe (screened in randomized order)
Comps/fundamentals: P/E, P/B, market cap, 52-wk range, avg volume on MU/ARM/SOXL/HOOD/MRVL/MRNA/AMD/TSM/ASML/SNOW
Catalysts: earnings calendar (MU 6/24-pm verified, EPS est $20.20) and May PCE 6/25; live quotes confirming the 6/23 semis reversal
Regime + MU setup: Warsh-Fed inflation focus, core PCE >3%, HBM-supercycle 'durable-or-cresting' framing, 6/23 'chips slammed' tape
data_examined.source: web_search (Schwab outlook + market update, IG/TechTimes MU previews)

analyst.generated_by: Claude for Financial Services (equity-research plugin methodology) via Claude Code, 2026-06-23 — de-risk-into-catalyst; universe screened in randomized order to remove position bias
analyst.note: Daily bars settled through the 2026-06-22 close; the agent book fills this tick's rebalance at those 6/22 closes (MU ~$1211, ARM ~$407, SOXL ~$300, HOOD ~$105). This is a de-risking tick: invested weight drops from ~85% to ~45% (MU 15% + HOOD 20% + MRNA 10%), with ~55% cash held as dry powder into the 6/24 MU print and 6/25 PCE. Sets the analyst's next-tick targets; the competitor book advances on the next completed session.

reflection.looking_back: Last tick I rebuilt the book around semis momentum into the 6/24 MU print — MU, ARM, SOXL, HOOD entered 6/18. Graded on settled data through 6/22 it was actually working: MU +6.8% and SOXL +7.6% from entry, partly offset by ARM -7.3% and HOOD -2.3%, leaving the book ~flat-to-slightly-up (equity ~$100.91, +0.9% all-time). The bet looked fine right up until the catalyst — which is exactly the trap a binary setup sets.
worked: Keeping HOOD as the non-semi diversifier: when the complex cracked on 6/23 it held up best (-2.4% vs -13%/-23% for MU/SOXL), validating it as the book's stabilizer.
Flagging MU's 6/24 print as the pivotal event and refusing to add ON TOP of the position into it.
missed: I carried ~65% in correlated semis (MU+ARM+SOXL) all the way to the print instead of trimming the correlation earlier — the prior reflection named this risk but I held the size a tick too long.

Leaning into ARM's 519x multiple and a 3x leveraged ETF was the highest-beta way to express the view; on a tape that cracked the day before the catalyst, those are the first things that should have been off.
reflection.adjustment: Execute the pre-committed plan now: treat MU/ARM/SOXL as one bet and cut it ahead of the print — exit the highest-beta/leveraged expressions (ARM, SOXL), halve the anchor (MU), keep the non-semi stabilizer (HOOD), and rebuild cash to ~55% so I can redeploy into clarity after the print rather than guess the print.

swarm.note: Live swarm — 150 fish, each a unique trader profile shown its own random ~20-name slice of the watchlist (with recent headlines), voted via OpenRouter. Independent-voter election; dissenters (ringed) chose CASH.

live.note: Connected to the Agentic cash account •••• — currently unfunded ($0). Deposit into this specific account to trade the real $100. No agent orders placed yet.

rules.momentum_breakout: Buy a 20-day-high breakout confirmed by above-average volume; exit when price closes below its 20-day SMA. Few big winners, many small losers.
rules.mean_reversion: Buy when RSI(14) < 30 (oversold); sell when RSI(14) > 70 (overbought). Trades less, wins more often, smaller edge.
rules.blended_momo_rsi: Momentum breakout, but skip entries already overbought (RSI ≥ 70); exit on trend break or RSI > 75. Avoids chasing extended moves.
rules.deep_research_analyst: Holds the analyst's target weights; rebalanced each tick from a fresh research report (web + Robinhood data).
rules.llm_voters: Allocates across the swarm's top vote-getters (weight ∝ vote share, capped at 60%); rebalanced each tick.
rules.mirofish_real: Real-MiroFish: persona agents with memory interact over rounds; consensus picks (weight ∝ vote share) rebalanced each tick.

sources.name: Standings, curves, decision trail (LIVE)
sources.detail: The forward paper test — every competitor trades a $100 book the same way, advanced one trading day per tick (rule strategies via tick.py / bot/engine.py; agents via run_agents.py / bot/paper.py), 5 bps slippage, −15% stop. Young — it grows one session per tick.

sources.name: Backtest reference
sources.detail: A separate Dec–Jun walk-forward backtest (2025-11-03–2026-06-22, in-sample, mostly-bull) is shown inside each rule strategy's detail for context only — it is NOT the live board.

sources.name: Research analyst
sources.detail: Agent-driven on the Claude Code plan: web_search + Robinhood data → one report (state/analyst.json) with target weights per tick. Sources link from each evidence row.

sources.name: Swarm vote
sources.detail: Independent-voter election: 150 unique-profile cheap models (DeepSeek/Gemini/Llama/Haiku via OpenRouter) each read one shared briefing and return a single ballot; tallied in bot/swarm.py. No interaction between fish.

sources.name: Real-MiroFish swarm
sources.detail: Social-simulation swarm (bot/mirofish.py): persona agents with memory interact over multiple rounds — each re-votes seeing its own prior view and its neighbours' latest — so a consensus forms via peer influence (the opposite of the independent swarm). Tiered cost (cheap/default/qwen); seeded from the briefing + optional world-events news.

sources.name: Live account
sources.detail: Robinhood MCP, Agentic cash account ••••, via get_portfolio / get_equity_positions / get_equity_orders.

sources.name: Display scale
sources.detail: The real paper books are $100 each; every dollar and share figure on this site is shown ×100 (a $10,000 book) so holdings read in whole shares and dollars. Per-share prices and percentages are unscaled.
